zh.lifeSummary: Anne Barre是John de la Bere的女兒，於1312年出生在英國賀福爾。她於1330年與William Devereux在英國英格蘭賀福爾賀福德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1394年在她的家鄉享年82歲。

zh-hans.Kington, Herefordshire, England, UK: 英国英格兰賀福爾金頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Anne Barre是John de la Bere的女儿，于1312年出生在英国賀福爾。她于1330年与William Devereux在英国英格兰賀福爾賀福德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1394年在她的家乡享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Anatole, Emile, Pierre, Alphonse, Andre, Cecile, Henri, Herve, Jacques, Marcel, Regean, Yves.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a gateway or barrier, or in a house encircled by a fence, from Old French barre ‘bar, obstruction’ (of obscure origin, possibly akin to the Celtic element barr ‘height’); or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, e.g. Barre-en-Ouche in Eure or Barre-de-Semilly in Manche. Compare Labarre .
French: probably also a habitational name from Barre in Lozère, named with the Celtic element barr ‘height’.

variants.name: Labarre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Alain, Andre, Armand, Aurore, Fernande, Gilles, Herve, Laure, Michel, Urbain.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a gateway or barrier, or in a house encircled by a fence, from Old French barre ‘bar, obstruction’ (see Barre 1), with fused feminine definite article la; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, e.g. Barre-en-Ouche in Eure or Barre-de-Semilly in Manche. Compare Labar , Labare , Labarr , Labor , Labore , Lebar , and Lebarre .
History: In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Jacques Genest or Genet dit Labarre from France (see Genest ).

variants.name: Baray
meanings: Hispanic (Mexico): unexplained.
In some cases possibly also French: variant of Barray, itself a variant of Barrey, a derivative of Barre 1. In France, it is found mainly in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Barrere
meanings: French (Barrère): topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or barrier, or a metonymic occupational name for a gatekeeper, from a derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1); or a habitational name from (La) Barrère, the name of several places in the southwestern part of France.
English: variant of Barrer, for someone who lived by a town or castle gate, from Middle English barre ‘barrier, gateway’. Compare above and Barr 4.

variants.name: Barriere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Gaston, Monique, Philippe.

French (Barrière): topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or barrier, or a metonymic occupational name for a gatekeeper, from a derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1); or a habitational name from (La) Barrière, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Gates .

variants.name: Barreau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Fresnel, Gabrielle, Jean-Louis, Jean-Marie, Jeanty, Michel, Michelene, Pierre.
French:

derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1), hence a nickname for a fence keeper or a topographic name for someone who lived in a house encircled by a fence.

variants.name: Barrier
meanings: French: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Old French barier, an agent derivative of bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1).
Altered form of French   Barriere  .
Altered form of German   Berger  .

variants.name: Barra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Marina, Antonio, Augusto, Biagio, Carlo, Domenic, Flavio, Gennaro, Lorenzo, Luciano, Rocco, Salvatore.  Spanish Jorge, Carlos, Diego, Enrique, Jaime, Juan, Manuel.
Italian (southern), Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name from barra ‘bar, rod’ (in the sense of ‘barrier, sandbank’), or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word.
French (southern): dialect variant of   Barre   1.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Anne Barre was born in 1312, in Herefordshire, England as the daughter of John de la Bere. She married William Devereux in 1330, in Hereford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1394, in her hometown, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Anne Barre est née en 1312, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, fille de John de la Bere. Elle a épousé William Devereux en 1330, à Hereford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1394, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Anne Barre wurde 1312 in Herefordshire, England, als Tochter von John de la Bere geboren. Sie hat 1330 in Hereford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom William Devereux geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1394 im Alter von 82 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Anne Barre è nata il nel 1312 nel Herefordshire, England come figlia di John de la Bere. Lei sposato William Devereux nel 1330, nel Hereford, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1394, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 82 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Anne Barreは1312年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでJohn de la Bereの娘として生まれた。1330年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアハーエフォードでWilliam Devereuxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1394年に故郷で82歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Anne Barre(은)는 1312년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 John de la Bere의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1330년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 허퍼드에서 William Devereux(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1394년 그녀의 고향에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Anne Barre nasceu em 1312, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra filha de John de la Bere. Ela casou-se com William Devereux em 1330, em Hereford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1394, em sua cidade natal, com 82 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Anne Barre родилась в 1312 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия. Её отца звали John de la Bere. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Devereux в 1330 г. в местности хирефорд, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1394 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 82 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Anne Barre nació en 1312, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra como hija de John de la Bere. Se casó con William Devereux en 1330, en Hereford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1394, en su pueblo, a la edad de 82 años.
